tasks.title: Codify malformed manifest contract and recovery path
tasks.goal: Make malformed manifest input fail deterministically with operator-facing recovery guidance before atomic persistence changes land.
scope: Define the canonical persisted source of truth between JSONL and CSV.
Surface malformed JSONL parse or validation failures with path and line context.
Document the operator recovery path for corrupted manifest state.
acceptance_criteria: Malformed manifest input fails fast instead of being silently skipped or partially loaded.
The surfaced error identifies the manifest path and the failing line or validation context with an actionable recovery hint.
The runbook states which manifest artifact is canonical and what operators should do when corruption is detected.
tasks.rollout: Land before atomic persistence so later changes can target one documented contract.
tasks.observability: Loader and service failures should mention the corrupted path without requiring debug mode.
tasks.notes: Assumption: JSONL remains the canonical manifest because runtime only reads manifest_jsonl_path today.

tasks.title: Implement atomic manifest writes for JSONL and CSV
tasks.goal: Replace direct manifest writes with temp-file-plus-replace persistence that preserves canonical files across interruptions.
scope: Add same-directory temp-file write helpers for JSONL and CSV outputs.
Update service persistence sequencing to align with the canonical-manifest contract.
Ensure failed writes do not leave partial canonical files behind.
acceptance_criteria: Manifest JSONL and CSV writes use temp-file-plus-replace semantics in the target directory.
A write failure does not leave a truncated canonical manifest file at either persisted path.
Service-level persistence sequencing follows the documented canonical-manifest contract.
tasks.rollout: Land after the loader contract is explicit so persistence ordering matches the documented recovery model.
tasks.observability: Persistence failures should be attributable to the specific target path that could not be committed.
tasks.notes: Implementation should minimize CSV/JSONL drift while prioritizing correctness of the canonical JSONL manifest.

tasks.title: Add manifest persistence regression coverage and operator guidance
tasks.goal: Lock in the corruption contract and atomic persistence behavior with focused tests and runbook updates.
scope: Add tests for malformed JSONL input and interrupted or failed persistence commits.
Verify service flows preserve canonical manifest state after failure scenarios.
Update operator documentation for corruption detection and recovery.
acceptance_criteria: Automated tests cover malformed manifest input with the documented error contract.
Automated tests cover at least one interrupted or failed write path without leaving partial canonical state behind.
The runbook explains how operators detect, recover from, and re-run after manifest corruption or persistence failure.
tasks.rollout: Complete this verification task before closing congreso-votaciones-445 or starting the dependent download-metadata work.
tasks.observability: Document the expected operator-visible error text and recovery steps.
tasks.notes: This task closes the loop between persistence semantics, user-facing guidance, and fixture-backed evidence.
